response.justification: While the candidate brings valuable geospatial data experience and strong software craftsmanship fundamentals, there are critical gaps for this mid-senior routing systems role. C++ expertise is outdated (last used 2014-2015) and current work is TypeScript/Node.js-focused, creating a significant technical reset risk. More importantly, the resume shows geospatial data collection and GIS mapping experience, not navigation routing or route optimization. The role requires someone immediately productive with modern C++ and navigation domain knowledge.
strengths: Extensive geospatial and GIS platform experience through multiple roles and open-source projects
Proven ability to build high-performance, low-latency systems handling millions of requests at Dunnhumby
Strong software craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, testing) demonstrated across many roles and open-source projects
concerns: C++ experience is over a decade old (2014-2015 at National Instruments); current primary stack is TypeScript/Node.js, representing a substantial skills gap
No demonstrated experience with navigation routing, route optimization, or navigation guidance systems—only data collection and mapping GIS platforms
Career trajectory shows movement away from systems programming toward web development and agent infrastructure, not toward the systems-level work this role requires
